name: overpy (OpenStreetMap Overpass API Client)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); DinoTools / PhiBo; Overpass API is free but rate-limited

auth.notes: No auth needed; Overpass API public endpoint; consider self-hosting for heavy use

performance.notes: Network-bound; Overpass server processing time varies with query complexity and load

agent_readiness.notes: Python client for OpenStreetMap Overpass API. import overpy. api = overpy.Overpass(). result = api.query('[out:json]; node["amenity"="cafe"](51.5,-0.2,51.6,-0.1); out;'). for node in result.nodes: print(node.lat, node.lon, node.tags.get('name')). result.ways, result.relations. Way: way.get_nodes(). Node: node.tags. Relations with roles. Custom endpoint: overpy.Overpass(url='https://overpass.myserver.org/api/interpreter'). max_retry_count, retry_timeout for resilience. OverPyException, MaxRetriesExceeded. Return format: json, xml, csv. Timeout in QL: [timeout:60].

gotchas: Overpass public API is rate-limited — large queries can get blocked; add delay between requests
Overpass QL (query language) must be learned — not standard SQL
Large area queries can time out on public Overpass servers
Result completeness depends on OSM data quality in the queried area
best_when: Querying OpenStreetMap data for POIs, roads, buildings by tag and bounding box
Geospatial data collection for specific OSM features (cafes, hospitals, roads)
Small to medium area queries without needing full OSM planet import
Combining with geopandas for spatial analysis of OSM features
avoid_when: Global or large-area queries — use osmnx or planet.osm instead
High-frequency production queries — self-host Overpass instance or use osmnx
When you need routing (use OpenRouteService or OSRM)
summary: overpy is an OpenStreetMap Overpass API client. api.query('[out:json]; node["amenity"="cafe"](bbox); out;'). result.nodes / ways / relations with lat, lon, tags. MIT by DinoTools. Public Overpass API rate-limited — add delays. Overpass QL required. Best for querying OSM POIs and features in bounded areas. No type annotations. Large queries timeout. Self-host for production use.
